featured_analysis.title: The Displacement Continuum
featured_analysis.description: A governance diagnostic for identifying where institutions stop following people across categorical, territorial, and temporal boundaries.

featured_analysis.title: The Fiscal Architecture of Displacement
featured_analysis.description: How sovereign finance, public expenditure systems, and institutional incentives shape displacement response.

publications.title: Governing Displacement: Institutional Fault Lines and Sustainable Inclusion
publications.abstract: Analysis of how institutional arrangements shape the transition from short-term response to sustainable inclusion.

publications.title: Development-Induced Displacement and State Absorption Capacity
publications.abstract: Explores how state systems absorb displacement pressures generated by development, infrastructure, and mobility transitions.

publications.title: Social Protection and Displacement
publications.abstract: Examines the role of national protection systems in supporting displaced populations and host communities.

publications.title: Fiscal Architecture and the Recurrent Obligation Problem
publications.abstract: Considers how public finance systems can account for the sustained costs of displacement inclusion.

commentary.title: Why mapping marks judgement-based cells
commentary.summary: A working diagnostic can be useful without pretending to be final. Judgement markers show where evidence still needs triangulation.

commentary.title: Displacement as a budget problem
commentary.summary: The policy question is not only whether assistance exists, but whether costs are carried by recurrent public systems.

commentary.title: Regional governance and ordinary systems
commentary.summary: Regional frameworks matter most when they shape how national systems include people in practice.

commentary.title: Development Finance Exposure
commentary.summary: Infrastructure and sovereign finance can produce or absorb displacement risk, but the risk is rarely priced explicitly.
